title: Democrats Clash Over Who Replaces Platner Even Before He Exits
summary: Progressives and moderates are gearing up for a fight over an as-yet-undecided process in Maine to name a Senate nominee to replace Graham Platner after a rape accusation. He still wants a say, too.

title: Bernie Sanders Calls on Graham Platner to Drop Senate Bid After Rape Allegation
summary: Mr. Sanders was one of the last remaining prominent allies of Graham Platner, the Maine Senate Democratic nominee, to call for him to drop out after a rape allegation.

title: The World Cup Stood as a Break From U.S. Politics. Then Trump Got Involved.
summary: The president’s call to the head of FIFA underscores that he has yet to find an area of American, or even global, life where he is unwilling to assert himself.

title: Trump Administration Guts Efforts to Prevent Gun Violence, Suppressing Reports
summary: As the administration works to expand gun rights, hospitals and community groups have lost funding, and reports on preventing firearm injuries have vanished from government websites.

title: After Courting Black Voters, Trump Downplays Black Unemployment
summary: The president has glossed over persistently high unemployment among African Americans, a group he needed to secure a second term.

title: Le Pen Says She Will Run for Presidency After Court Lifts Ban
summary: An appeals court confirmed Marine Le Pen’s embezzlement conviction, but shortened a ban on her eligibility for elected office. That means the French far-right leader can run for president in 2027.

title: The Accused Is in Court but Conspiracy Theories Still Swirl Around Kirk Case
summary: Mr. Kirk’s assassination played out live on the internet, but since then, it has become fodder for fantasy.

title: Venezuelans Criticize Own Government Response After Twin Earthquakes
summary: Venezuelans are publicly expressing their anger over their government’s disaster response, despite the risk of imprisonment.

title: Businesses Fear Politicization as Trump Gains More Power Over U.S. Agencies
summary: A Supreme Court ruling that presidents can fire independent regulators without cause has added volatility for industries that prefer stable enforcement.

title: A Supreme Court Case on AR-15s Could Hinge on Their ‘Common Use’
summary: Last week, the Supreme Court took up challenges to two assault weapons bans. Its decision could seriously limit the government’s ability to ban certain guns.

title: The Disappearing Las Vegas Buffets Hold a Mirror to the American Soul
summary: As a once-grand American tradition enters its twilight, our critic journeys to the desert to find out what it all means.

title: Estonia Won the War on Fentanyl. What Came Next Was Even Worse.
summary: By 2018, fentanyl overdoses in Estonia had plummeted. But powerful new drugs are appearing fast, with the authorities racing to respond.

title: The 39-ish Most Stylish People of 2026, So Far
summary: On red carpets, on set, onstage and even up in space, they captured our attention with their looks.

title: I Wanted an Ecologically Responsible Garden. It Was Harder Than I Thought.
summary: The native plant movement gets a lot right, but there’s so much more to consider.

title: America Was Being Played. The Bessent Doctrine Says Those Days Are Over.
summary: The United States is changing the rules of trade in its favor.

title: JD Vance Is Worried
summary: The vice president’s theology comes in book form.

title: A Supreme Leader’s Final Crowds
summary: What you could see when Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s coffin was being driven to a public space in Tehran.
